meta.name: Creamy Chicken and Rice Soup
meta.subtitle: Thick, filling, and family-friendly • great for batch cooking

source.notes: SSA baseline designed for 'use what you have' logic: rotisserie/leftover chicken, optional veggies, dairy or dairy-free creamy finish.

yield.notes: Makes ~14–16 cups depending on simmer and rice absorption.

tasteProfile.notes: Classic chicken soup comfort with creamy body, tender rice, and gentle aromatics.

ingredients.label: Butter or olive oil
ingredients.notes: About 2 tablespoons. Use butter for classic flavor.

ingredients.label: All-purpose flour
ingredients.notes: About 1/4 cup. Forms a roux for a creamy base.

ingredients.label: Chicken broth or stock (low-sodium preferred)
ingredients.notes: About 8 cups. Use stock for richer body.

ingredients.label: Whole milk
ingredients.notes: About 2 cups. Add after thickening; keep gentle heat.

ingredients.label: Heavy cream (optional richer finish)
ingredients.notes: About 1/2 cup. Can replace part of the milk.

ingredients.label: Unsweetened coconut milk (optional dairy-free finish)
ingredients.notes: About 1 1/3 cups. If used, omit milk/cream and add at the end.

ingredients.label: White rice, uncooked (long-grain preferred)
ingredients.notes: About 1 cup. Long-grain stays less sticky; see planner notes for alternatives.

ingredients.label: Cooked chicken, shredded or diced
ingredients.notes: About 3–4 cups. Great use for rotisserie or leftover roast chicken.

ingredients.label: Salt
ingredients.notes: Start with ~1 1/4 teaspoons; adjust at end based on broth.

ingredients.label: Frozen peas (optional)
ingredients.notes: Stir in at the end for color/sweetness.

steps.title: Sweat aromatics
instructions: Melt butter (or heat oil) in a large pot over medium heat.
Add onion, carrots, and celery. Cook 8–10 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and cook 30 seconds until fragrant.

steps.title: Make the roux (thickening base)
instructions: Sprinkle flour over the vegetables and stir well.
Cook 1–2 minutes, stirring, to remove raw flour taste (do not let it brown heavily).

steps.title: Add broth and simmer
instructions: Slowly pour in the broth while stirring to prevent lumps.
Add thyme/Italian seasoning and bay leaf (optional).
Bring to a gentle simmer and cook 10 minutes to thicken slightly.

steps.title: Cook the rice
instructions: Simmer 15–18 minutes (or until rice is tender), stirring occasionally so rice doesn’t stick.

steps.title: Add chicken and creamy finish
instructions: Lower heat. Stir in cooked chicken.
Stir in milk (and cream if using). Warm gently 3–5 minutes—avoid a hard boil after adding dairy.
If using coconut milk instead (dairy-free), add it now and warm gently.

steps.title: Season and serve
instructions: Remove bay leaf (if used). Taste and adjust salt and pepper.
Finish with parsley (optional) and a small squeeze of lemon juice (optional) for brightness.

swaps: Rice: long-grain white is most stable; jasmine works; brown rice needs longer cook; wild rice blend needs longer and more liquid.
Chicken: any cooked chicken works; leftover turkey also works.
Vegetables: add diced potato, corn, green beans, or spinach if you have them (add tender greens at the end).
thicknessControl: Soup thickens as rice sits. Loosen with extra broth or water when reheating.
For extra-thick, slightly increase flour to 45g or simmer uncovered a few minutes after adding milk.

bestPractices: For best freezing, consider cooking rice separately and adding to bowls when serving (prevents over-soft rice after thaw).
If freezing the full soup, freeze before adding dairy; add milk/cream when reheating for best texture.

familyFriendlyNotes: Keep seasoning gentle; offer hot sauce/pepper at the table for adults.
Dice vegetables small for kid-friendly texture.

storage.fridge: Refrigerate up to 4 days. Soup thickens as rice absorbs liquid; add broth/water on reheat.
storage.freezer: Freeze up to 3 months. Best results: freeze without dairy, and/or keep rice separate for texture.
storage.reheat: Reheat gently to a simmer, stirring often. Avoid hard boiling after adding dairy.

notes: If you prefer a super-smooth creamy base, you can briefly blend a portion of the soup (before adding chicken) and then stir it back in—optional technique.
Low-sodium broth helps prevent over-salting; always final-season at the end.

audit.testNotes: Schema-compliant: schemaVersion+meta present; ingredients include id+label+amount; steps include id+title+instructions; no disallowed custom top-level properties.
